author: Socrates
text: The unexamined life is not worth living.

author: Aristotle
text: For the things we have to learn before we can do them, we learn by doing them.

author: Marcus Aurelius
text: You have power over your mind—not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength.

author: Marcus Aurelius
text: The happiness of your life depends upon the quality of your thoughts.

author: Epictetus
text: Men are disturbed not by things, but by the views which they take of things.

author: Confucius
text: Our greatest glory is not in never falling, but in rising every time we fall.

author: Lao Tzu
text: A journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step.

author: Plato
text: The beginning is the most important part of the work.

author: Francis Bacon
text: Read not to contradict and confute; nor to believe and take for granted; nor to find talk and discourse; but to weigh and consider.

author: Francis Bacon
text: Some books are to be tasted, others to be swallowed, and some few to be chewed and digested.

author: William Shakespeare
text: To be, or not to be, that is the question.

author: William Shakespeare
text: This above all: to thine own self be true.

author: William Shakespeare
text: We know what we are, but know not what we may be.

author: Jane Austen
text: It is a truth universally acknowledged, that a single man in possession of a good fortune, must be in want of a wife.

author: Charles Dickens
text: It was the best of times, it was the worst of times.

author: Ralph Waldo Emerson
text: To believe your own thought, to believe that what is true for you in your private heart is true for all men—that is genius.

author: Henry David Thoreau
text: I went to the woods because I wished to live deliberately, to front only the essential facts of life.

author: Leo Tolstoy
text: All happy families are alike; each unhappy family is unhappy in its own way.

author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
text: The mystery of human existence lies not in just staying alive, but in finding something to live for.

author: Victor Hugo
text: Even the darkest night will end and the sun will rise.

author: Oscar Wilde
text: We are all in the gutter, but some of us are looking at the stars.

author: Emily Dickinson
text: Hope is the thing with feathers that perches in the soul.

author: Walt Whitman
text: Do I contradict myself? Very well then I contradict myself, I am large, I contain multitudes.

author: Herman Melville
text: It is not down on any map; true places never are.

author: Friedrich Nietzsche
text: That which does not kill us makes us stronger.

author: Mary Shelley
text: Nothing is so painful to the human mind as a great and sudden change.

author: Louisa May Alcott
text: I am not afraid of storms, for I am learning how to sail my ship.

author: Charlotte Brontë
text: I am no bird; and no net ensnares me: I am a free human being with an independent will.

author: Seneca
text: It is not that we have a short time to live, but that we waste a lot of it.

author: Homer
text: There is a time for many words, and there is also a time for sleep.
